title_en: Persecution of the Muslims in Mecca
content_en:
As the Prophet's ﷺ message gained traction and the number of Muslims grew, the Quraysh leadership's tolerance gave way to systematic persecution. Al-Mubarakpuri's account of this period — which lasted approximately ten years from the first public call until the Hijra — is one of the most moving sections of al-Rahiq al-Makhtum, documenting the extraordinary suffering endured by the early Muslim community and the steadfastness with which they bore it.

The persecution targeted primarily those without tribal protection — slaves, freed men, foreigners, and converts whose tribes did not support their new faith. The most severe early cases included Bilal ibn Rabah, an Abyssinian slave owned by Umayyah ibn Khalaf, who was dragged into the scorching desert and made to lie on the burning sand with a heavy stone on his chest, told he would not be released until he renounced Muhammad ﷺ. Bilal's response — 'Ahad, Ahad' (One, One) — repeated through his agony, has become one of the most iconic testimonies of faith in Islamic history. Abu Bakr eventually purchased and freed him.

Ammar ibn Yasir and his parents, Yasir and Sumayyah, were among the most severely tortured families. They were poor and had no tribal protection. Sumayyah bint Khabbat was murdered by Abu Jahl — the first martyr in Islamic history. Yasir died under torture. Ammar was forced to make statements of disbelief under extreme duress, and when he came to the Prophet ﷺ weeping and confessing what he had said, the Prophet ﷺ asked gently: 'How is your heart?' When Ammar confirmed his faith was firm, the Prophet ﷺ recited the verse: 'Except for one who is compelled while his heart is firm in faith' (Quran 16:106) — one of the foundational texts for the Islamic doctrine that coerced speech does not constitute apostasy.

Khabbab ibn al-Arat was another who endured severe torture — he was made to lie on burning coals and the fire was extinguished only by the melting fat of his own back. Countless others — Suhayb al-Rumi, Abu Fukayhah, and many unnamed slaves — suffered similarly.

The persecution of those with tribal protection, though less physically violent, was severe in other ways. A social and economic boycott (muqata'ah) was eventually declared against the entire clan of Banu Hashim — whether Muslim or not — confining them to a mountain pass called Shi'b Abi Talib for approximately three years, during which they were denied trade, food, and social interaction until they were reduced to eating leaves. This boycott, documented on a parchment hung in the Kaaba, was eventually broken when several Meccan leaders of conscience discovered that the parchment had been eaten by white ants except for the words 'In Your name, O Allah.'

The Prophet ﷺ himself was subject to ridicule, harassment, and physical abuse — having filth thrown on him during prayer, having thorns placed in his path, and being choked by Abu Jahl. Through all of this, his response was patience, continued preaching, and supplication to Allah for guidance of his community — not vengeance. This period forged in the early Muslim community a spiritual resilience and a depth of commitment that would define the character of Islamic civilization for centuries to come.
title_id: Penganiayaan terhadap Kaum Muslim di Makkah
content_id:
Ketika pesan Nabi ﷺ semakin mendapat sambutan dan jumlah kaum Muslim bertambah, toleransi pimpinan Quraisy berubah menjadi penganiayaan yang sistematis. Al-Mubarakpuri mendokumentasikan periode ini — yang berlangsung sekitar sepuluh tahun dari seruan pertama secara terbuka hingga Hijrah — dengan mencatat kisah-kisah ketahanan luar biasa dari kaum Muslim awal yang menghadapi siksaan fisik, pemboikotan ekonomi, dan tekanan sosial untuk meninggalkan agama baru mereka.

Orang-orang yang paling menderita adalah mereka yang tidak memiliki perlindungan suku — budak dan orang-orang miskin. Bilal bin Rabah (ra) disiksa oleh tuannya Umayyah bin Khalaf dengan cara ditindih batu besar di bawah terik matahari gurun, namun ia terus berteriak: "Ahad! Ahad! (Allah Satu! Allah Satu!)" hingga Abu Bakr (ra) membelinya dan memerdekakannya. Keluarga Yasir — Ammar, ayahnya Yasir, dan ibunya Sumayyah (ra) — adalah keluarga pertama yang menanggung syahid; Sumayyah (ra) dibunuh oleh Abu Jahal, menjadikannya syahidah pertama dalam sejarah Islam.

Para pemimpin Quraisy menggunakan setiap cara untuk menghentikan dakwah: bujukan (menawarkan kekayaan dan kekuasaan kepada Nabi ﷺ), intimidasi, dan akhirnya kekerasan fisik terhadap para pengikutnya. Namun setiap upaya mereka justru memperkuat tekad kaum Muslim.

Nabi ﷺ sendiri tidak terbebas dari penganiayaan: beliau dilempari kotoran, dihalang-halangi dari shalat di Ka'bah, dan bahkan diancam pembunuhan. Namun beliau bersabar, mendoakan kaumnya, dan terus menyampaikan risalah dengan penuh keteguhan.
